title: STAT+: ‘Secret shopper’ study probes GLP-1 telehealth sites
summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: A 'secret shopper' study of telehealth sites prescribing GLP-1s, news about Epic, Anthropic, and brain implants.

title: Vertex to acquire Crinetics in $10B foray into endocrine disease drugs
summary: Vertex claimed the two drugs in the deal could eventually bring in more than $5 billion annually. Still, the high price it paid stirred debate among analysts and investors.

summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals will spend $10 billion to acquire Crinetics Pharmaceuticals and its drug for a rare endocrine disorder

title: 5 takeaways from STAT’s series on the soaring cost of health insurance
summary: Read the 5 key takeaways from STAT's series "Out of Pocket, Out of Reach," an examination of workplace health insurance.

title: Opinion: What if this Ebola outbreak can’t be stopped?
summary: In the past, the possibility of endemic Ebola was unthinkable. This time is different, write Ivan Buendia Gayton and Eric D. Perakslis.

title: STAT+: This whistleblower took on a health insurance giant and a political machine. She’s not stopping there
summary: America’s employers must stop acting helpless in the face of health care industry profiteering, Chris Deacon says.

title: STAT+: America’s small businesses are giving up on health insurance
summary: America's employer-based health insurance system is crumbling. STAT investigates why, in our new series, “Out of Pocket, Out of Reach.”

title: STAT+: For a Georgia entrepreneur, health coverage has never been affordable
summary: Amy Bielawski is one of the 30 million people who run their own businesses. She's gone most of her life without health insurance.

title: Opinion: Medicine thinks Gen Z is too soft. It’s wrong
summary: “Gen Z physicians are on a collision course with institutions still built around older assumptions,” writes Frantz M. Berthaud.

title: STAT+: Vertex acquires Crinetics Pharmaceuticals for $10 billion as biotech M&A booms
summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals will spend $10 billion to acquire Crinetics Pharmaceuticals and its drug for a rare endocrine disorder, the companies said.

title: Obamacare rolls shrank dramatically in many states over the past year, new federal data shows
summary: States across the country saw steep drops in the number of people covered by the Affordable Care Act over the past year, according to new federal data.

title: STAT+: Online GLP-1 prescriptions are often fast, easy — and low on clinical oversight
summary: A new secret-shopper study found that GLP-1 prescriptions from telehealth sites aren't often backed by appropriate clinical care.

title: Gene editing developer Scribe plots an IPO
summary: The California drugmaker, which has collaborations in place with Biogen, Sanofi and Eli Lilly, could be the 14th biotech to go public in 2026.

title: Novartis to acquire ADC developer in a deal worth up to $1.5B
summary: The deal hands over two ADCs with a different mechanism of action targeting cell growth, and marks Novartis' entry into ADC development.

summary: Anthropic CEO talks drug discovery, Medicare proposes steep 340B drug payment cuts, and more biotech news

title: Cancer, immune drugmakers dominate VC funding so far in 2026
summary: Drug developers focusing on either disease area made up more than 40% of the number of biotech companies and amount raised so far in 2026, per BioPharma Dive data.

title: Beyond Boston: Unlikely cities stake biotech claims
summary: As Greater Boston’s biotech industry sheds jobs, three unlikely regions are showing promise as up-and-coming hubs.

summary: Supporters say clinical trial diversity is about making sure drugs and devices work, not about DEI.

title: Opinion: Stopping doctors from ordering unnecessary diagnostic tests requires a structural fix
summary: “The medical industry has spent enormous effort making more advanced tests but expended little effort learning how to use tests correctly,” writes Daniel Morgan.

summary: “The FDA should pursue a structured, clinician-guided framework” on popular peptides, writes former surgeon general Jerome Adams.

title: What does content creation do to mental health?
summary: This year at Vidcon, I was struck by how much of the conference’s programming was dedicated to the mental health of content creators.

title: HuR degrader knocks down BRAF in cancer

summary: Celea, backed by investors such as RA Capital and Leaps by Bayer, is making what it says is an improved version of Roche's Esbriet.

title: Roche KRAS drug succeeds in head-to-head lung cancer study
summary: The Phase 3 trial compared Roche’s divarasib with treatments sold by Amgen and Bristol Myers in patients with non-small cell lung cancer.

summary: Elsewhere, Otsuka’s kidney drug hit the goal of a confirmatory trial and Vertex’s CRISPR sickle cell drug secured expanded approval.

title: Ipsen to buy Swiss biotech Memo Therapeutics in potential $800M deal
summary: The purchase hands Ipsen an experimental medicine in late-stage testing for a virus that often affects kidney transplant patients.

summary: A group of coming decisions could provide important insights into how flexible the agency is willing to be under new leadership.
